rulings.Case Title: ANGELO DATIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 11, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant United Refrigeration, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to its affirmative defense of sophisticated user, defendant did not sustain its burden of production because it did not present any evidence that all of plaintiff¿s exposure to asbestos-containing products sold or supplied by defendant occurred after plaintiff knew or should have known that those products contained asbestos and that the asbestos in those products posed a safety hazard to plaintiff. As to plaintiff¿s claims for negligence and false representation and his request for punitive damages, defendant has not satisfied its burden of production that plaintiff does not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to prove these matters. Purported undisputed material fact 30 in defendant¿s separate statement makes no reference to what was stated by plaintiff in his responses to defendant¿s ¿case specific discovery,¿ much less anything to suggest those responses were ¿factually devoid¿ within the meaning of Aguilar. Defendant¿s purported undisputed material facts 32 and 34 state that plaintiff served only objections to special interrogatories seeking information about plaintiff¿s claim for false representation and his request for punitive damages. Objections, without more, are not a basis for a court to conclude that a plaintiff is unable to prove what he needs to prove to prevail. (Gaggero v. Yura (2003) 108 Cal. App. 4th 884, 889-92). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS HIXSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant D.W. Nicholson Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s deposition testimony are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHORTALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Nibco Inc.¿s motion to strike amendment to complaint is granted. Plaintiff was not ignorant of Nibco at the time he filed his complaint. While it may be rue that plaintiff may add Nibco as a defendant through other means, the sole issue on this motion is whether plaintiff may add Nibco per CCP 474. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP DUNCAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Fraser¿s Boiler Service, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issue 3 (premises owner/contractor liability) and taken off calendar as to issues 1, 2, 4 and 5 (strict liability, false representation, civil battery and punitive damages). As pled, plaintiff¿s cause of action for premises owner/contractor liability, as it relates to contractors, does not require a showing that defendant owned, operated, maintained or controlled the premises. While this likely means that there is no substantive difference between plaintiffs¿ causes of action for negligence and premises owner/contractor liability, that is not a basis to grant summary adjudication and is more appropriately handled by the trial judge through jury instructions or otherwise. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for strict liability, false representation and civil battery and his request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CLARENCE DELOUIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Core Industries, Inc.¿s amended motion for summary judgment is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to give proper notice required by C.C.P. § 437c. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R. LEONARD et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s motion to strike is taken off calendar as moot in light of the tentative ruling on defendant¿s demurrer. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R. LEONARD et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant John Crane, Inc.¿s demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action (loss of consortium) and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first and second cause of action (negligence and strict liability). Since all of Mr. Leonard¿s claimed exposure to asbestos pre-dated his marriage with Mrs. Leonard, Mrs. Leonard does not have a viable claim for loss of consortium. Plaintiffs¿ argument that Zwicker is inapplicable is belied by the language of Zwicker. (See also Owens-Illinois, Inc. v. Cook (MD 2005) 872 A.2d 969, 982-84 (an asbestos exposure case which refers to Zwicker as among those cases that hold that ¿the marriage requirement for a loss of consortium claim is absolute, [and] that if the marriage post-dated the injury, there is no consortium claim.¿)). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSS et al VS. C.C. MOORE & CO. ENGINEERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Plaintiffs¿ motion for order granting leave to file second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)
